_document_type: Jurisdiction regulatory lookup cache — staged so the Phase 3 jurisdiction scan resolves instantly for the demo asset
_note: Figures below are illustrative and rounded for demo purposes (dates, thresholds and the general penalty mechanism reflect the real Energize Denver ordinance structure; do not cite the exact per-sqft penalty rate to a client without confirming the current year's published schedule).

jurisdiction.name: City and County of Denver, Colorado

applicable_regulations.scope: Existing commercial and multifamily buildings > 25,000 sqft
applicable_regulations.mechanism: Building-specific EUI (kBtu/sqft/yr) targets set on a declining schedule toward a 2030 final target; annual benchmarking required via Denver's Energize Denver portal (Energy Star Portfolio Manager feed).

target_schedule.status: At risk — ~14% above interim target absent DHW electrification
At risk — Phase 1+2 decarb plan required to close gap

penalty_structure.mechanism: Escalating per-sqft annual penalty for each year a covered building exceeds its target EUI, assessed retroactively to the compliance year
penalty_structure.compliance_pathway: Timeline Extension or Alternative Compliance Plan available if a capital improvement plan (e.g., the Phase 1+2 decarb measures) is filed with the city before the target year

applicable_regulations.current_status: Compliant with 2024 benchmarking requirement; 2027 interim target at risk without the DHW electrification measure in the decarb plan

applicable_regulations.regulation: Colorado HB21-1286 — Building Energy Performance for large buildings
applicable_regulations.scope: Statewide benchmarking + performance standard for buildings > 50,000 sqft; supplements/aligns with Energize Denver for covered properties
applicable_regulations.current_status: Compliant — covered by Energize Denver benchmarking submission, which satisfies the state's reporting requirement for buildings within Denver city limits

applicable_regulations.regulation: IRA — Inflation Reduction Act clean energy provisions
applicable_regulations.scope: Applies nationwide; relevant incentive pathways for this deal: §48E Investment Tax Credit (solar, heat pump water heater), MACRS accelerated depreciation
applicable_regulations.relevance: Directly reduces net CapEx for the DHW electrification and solar measures in the decarb plan (~30% ITC)

utility_incentive_programs.requirement: Projected >=25% reduction in combined energy and water consumption from planned capital measures
